prompt: Which department offers the Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Computer science and Engineering (CSE) program?
response: The Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Computer science and Engineering (CSE) program is offered by the Department of Computer Science and Engineering.

prompt: What is the total credit requirement for Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Computer science and Engineering (CSE)?
response: The total requirement for Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Computer science and Engineering (CSE) is 140 credits.

prompt: How are the credits distributed in the Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Computer science and Engineering (CSE) curriculum?
response: In Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Computer science and Engineering (CSE), credits are typically distributed as follows: Compulsory Language and General Education Courses — 9 credits; Elective General Education Courses — 9 credits; Compulsory Basic Science Courses — 11 credits; Compulsory Mathematics and Statistics Courses — 15 credits; Core Computer Science and Engineering Courses — 62 credits; Core Capstone Project — 6 credits; Major Compulsory and Elective Computer Science and Engineering Courses — 20 credits; Non-major Elective Computer Science and Engineering Courses — 8 credits.

prompt: What electives can I take in the Intelligent Systems and Data Science track of Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Computer science and Engineering (CSE)?
